title: lonchocarpus emarginatus
intro: Lonchocarpus emarginatus, also known as the "Pangpang" or "Pangpang tree", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a deciduous tree that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus emarginatus include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-ulcer agent. Herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus emarginatus are typically made from its bark, leaves, and seeds, which can be used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or topical creams and ointments.
